title: Joint hyperscaler outage (AWS + GCP + Azure)
rationale: Upper-bound scenario: all three hyperscalers unreachable simultaneously (e.g., a shared certificate authority compromise or coordinated denial of service against TLS-fronting Akamai).
top_entities: Coinbase / bison.run (2378M); CHUCK BUX (704M); Binance (655M); Wave / Wavepool (611M); Upbit (604M)

title: Correlated AWS + GCP outage
rationale: Joint failure scenario: an AWS us-east-1 incident that propagates to GCP through shared DNS / IAM / CA upstreams. This crosses the 25% quorum heuristic and is the most plausible threshold-breaching event observed in the data.
top_entities: Coinbase / bison.run (2378M); CHUCK BUX (704M); Wave / Wavepool (611M); Upbit (604M); Binance (595M)

title: Complete AWS unavailability (control plane + all regions)
rationale: Unprecedented at this scope but used as upper-bound: full AWS absence (BGP withdrawal, root-DNS misconfiguration, or certificate-authority chain failure).
top_entities: Coinbase / bison.run (1250M); CHUCK BUX (704M); Upbit (604M); Binance (595M); YUTA (452M)

title: Complete OVH outage (multi-datacentre)
rationale: OVH-wide event such as a billing/identity outage affecting all OVH datacentres simultaneously. OVH is the largest non-hyperscaler concentration on Cardano.

title: GCP global IAM / control-plane outage
rationale: Multi-region GCP control-plane events (Jun 2019, Nov 2021, Mar 2024) have made every GCP region simultaneously unreachable for authenticated workloads.
top_entities: Coinbase / bison.run (1128M); Wave / Wavepool (611M); NuFi (300M); Blockdaemon (249M); MS4 (139M)

title: OVH Strasbourg datacentre fire (SBG-style)
rationale: Real precedent: March 2021, SBG2 destroyed by fire; SBG1, SBG3, SBG4 went offline for days. Predicate matches OVH stake whose physical datacentre is in France (Roubaix / Strasbourg / Gravelines), excluding OVH's foreign datacentres (Beauharnois, Hillsboro, Singapore, Sydney) which BGP-register to the FR/CA parent block but are not exposed to a Strasbourg-region event.

title: Frankfurt metro-area outage (DE-CIX peering point)
rationale: DE-CIX (Frankfurt) is one of the largest Internet exchanges; a metro-level power or peering failure affects every datacentre in the Frankfurt cluster simultaneously, regardless of provider.
top_entities: Coinbase / bison.run (988M); Everstake (582M); SECUR (239M); AdaOcean (185M); NEDS1 (112M)

title: Complete Hetzner outage
rationale: Hetzner (Nuremberg / Falkenstein / Helsinki) is the densest single-pool-operator hoster. A Hetzner-wide event silences a large slice of the long tail.

title: Single-region worst case (GCP europe-west3 / Frankfurt)
rationale: The largest single-region concentration observed in the data. Dominated by Coinbase. Bounded by what any one region failure can cost the network.
top_entities: Coinbase / bison.run (988M); RAID (0M); VKP (0M); pool1nq0jx33p0 (0M); pool190vx20f4p (0M)

title: Azure westeurope (Amsterdam) regional outage
rationale: Azure westeurope hosts essentially all eToro stake plus other European institutional operators; the region's storage + AAD incidents (Apr 2021, May 2022) silence everything there.

title: AWS us-east-1 control-plane cascade
rationale: Historically the most consequential AWS region; control-plane events in 2017, 2020 and 2021 cascaded across other AWS regions and into GCP / Azure via shared IAM, STS, and DNS dependencies.
